title: Subtree of Another Tree
description: Given the roots of two binary trees root and subRoot, return true if there is a subtree of root with the same structure and node values as subRoot, and false otherwise. A subtree of a binary tree is a node of that tree plus all of its descendants. The tree root itself is also a subtree.
constraints: The number of nodes in the root tree is in the range [1, 2000]
The number of nodes in the subRoot tree is in the range [1, 1000]
thinking_questions: How does this relate to the Same Tree problem?
At each node of the main tree, you could check if the subtree rooted there is the same as subRoot. What's the complexity?
Can you serialize both trees and use string matching for O(m+n)?

hints.L1: For each node in the main tree, check if the tree rooted there is identical to subRoot. Use your isSameTree function.
hints.L2: DFS: if root is None return False. If isSameTree(root, subRoot) return True. Else recurse on left and right.

follow_up_questions: What's the string serialization approach and its complexity?
How does the order of DFS matter — should you check isSameTree before or after recursing?
What if both trees are huge — is O(m·n) acceptable?
solution_notes: DFS on root; at each node call isSameTree(node, subRoot). O(m·n). String serialization gives O(m+n).
